channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: UN: Sudan war is world's worst humanitarian crisis: 30 million need aid, 16 million of them kids
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The war in Sudan has created the world’s largest and most devastating humanitarian crisis with over 30 million people needing aid this year, 16 million of them children trying to survive in dire conditions, the head of the U.N. children’s agency said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN report accuses Israel of sexual violence against Palestinians. Netanyahu claims anti-Israel bias
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — United Nations-backed human rights experts on Thursday accused Israel of “the systematic use of sexual, reproductive and other gender-based violence” in its war with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Peru's detained ex-President Castillo transferred to a hospital days after starting hunger strike
item.description: Former Peruvian President Pedro Castillo has been taken from prison to a hospital days after beginning a hunger strike in protest of his ongoing rebellion trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pakistan's leader meets survivors of deadly train hijacking and the commandos who ended the siege
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistan’s prime minister on Thursday commended the country’s armed forces for successfully rescuing 339 passengers after a deadly train hijacking by insurgents in the southwest. A total of 25 people were killed by the attackers.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration empties Guantánamo of migrants, flies all back to US
item.description: The Trump administration’s plans to detain migrants in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, hit another roadblock this week after all 40 such individuals held there were sent back to the United States, with no future deportation flights scheduled to the island.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration empties Guantánamo of migrants, flies all back to US
item.description: The Trump administration’s plans to detain migrants in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, hit another roadblock this week after all 40 such individuals held there were sent back to the United States, with no future deportation flights scheduled to the island.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Syrian leader signs constitution that puts the country under an Islamist group's rule for 5 years
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Syria's interim president on Thursday signed a temporary constitution that leaves the country under Islamist rule for five years during a transitional phase.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin adviser says ceasefire gives nothing to Russia, would only help Ukraine
item.description: An adviser to Russian President Vladimir Putin said a U.S.-proposed 30-day ceasefire gave nothing to Russia and only benefited Ukraine, in one of Moscow’s first public signs of opposition to a deal Kyiv agreed to on Tuesday. Yuri Ushakov, a former Russian ambassador to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Belgian prosecutors arrest suspects in Huawei bribery probe targeting EU Parliament
item.description: Belgian federal prosecutors say they've arrested several people as part of a corruption probe linked to the European Parliament and Huawei.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia presents US with demands that need to be met before ending Ukraine war: report
item.description: Russia has reportedly laid out its demands for ending the war in Ukraine and they mirror previous requests made to Kyiv, the U.S. and NATO.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top diplomats from G7 countries meet in Canada as Trump threatens more tariffs on US allies
item.description: Top diplomats from Group of 7 industrialized democracies have gathered in Canada as U.S. President Donald Trump's trade and foreign policies have thrown the bloc's once solid unity into disarray.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: They crossed the world to reach the US. Now deported under Trump, they're stuck in Panama
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — They crossed oceans to get to the U.S., fleeing conflict, religious persecution, poverty and government crackdowns in countries such as Afghanistan, Somalia, Cameroon, China, Pakistan and Iran.
item.dc:creator: MATÍAS DELACROIX and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congo and M23 rebel group will hold peace talks on Tuesday, mediator Angola says
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — The government of Congo will hold peace talks next week in Angola with the Rwanda-backed M23 rebel group that has captured key areas of Congo’s mineral-rich east, mediator Angola announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US puts Putin in hot seat with Ukraine ceasefire proposal
item.description: The United States has for the first time shifted the pressure to Russia in President Trump’s push for a ceasefire in Ukraine, after securing Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s support for a 30-day truce in the war.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US and Russian spy chiefs speak amid Ukraine war talks
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe and Sergei Naryshkin, chief of the Russian Foreign Intelligence Service, or SVR, spoke over the phone Tuesday amid recent peace talks with Ukraine, endorsing a 30-day ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Pressed to accept a ceasefire in Ukraine, Putin seems likely to seek his own conditions
item.description: By signaling its openness to a ceasefire, Ukraine has handed the Kremlin a difficult challenge at a time when the Russian military has the upper hand in the war: Should Moscow accept a truce and abandon hopes of making new gains, or should it reject the offer and risk derailing a cautious rapprochem
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says Canada takeover won't be on G7 agenda
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that talk about the U.S. acquiring Canada will not be a topic of discussion when the Group of Seven (G7) nations meet in Quebec.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Russia hits Zelenskyy's hometown as Ukraine signals it's ready for peace
item.description: As Ukraine appeared to pivot towards peace, Russia launched a deadly attack on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown of Kryvvi Rih.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Detained captain of cargo ship that collided with US oil tanker is Russian national, ship owner says
item.description: A Russian national was revealed to be the captain of the cargo ship that collided with an American tanker off the coast of England, spilling U.S. military jet fuel into the sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia waiting for US-Ukraine cease-fire proposal details, Kremlin says
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov told reporters Wednesday that Russia must get the details on U.S.-Ukrainian cease-fire talks before commenting on the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenland's election winners push back against Trump's wish to take control of the island
item.description: A party that favors a gradual path to Greenland’s independence from Denmark has won a surprise victory in parliamentary elections.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope Francis follows Vatican spiritual retreat as doctors say he's no longer in imminent danger
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis participated remotely in the Vatican's spiritual retreat Tuesday after getting good news from his doctors: They upgraded his prognosis and say he is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of the respiratory infection that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a mon
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills 8 Palestinians in Gaza as Hamas says ceasefire talks resume
item.description: Israeli fire has killed eight Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and three more in the occupied West Bank over the past 24 hours, Palestinian officials said Tuesday, even as a fragile ceasefire with Hamas in Gaza has largely held since late January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Search for Sudiksha Konanki, missing Pitt student, continues in Dominican Republic
item.description: Sudiksha Konanki was last seen early Thursday morning on a beach near Punta Cana. Police have spoken with a man allegedly with her at the time.
item.dc:creator: Nick Smith, NewsNation

item.title: US, Ukraine talk peace as Kyiv, Moscow trade drone attacks
item.description: Officials from Ukraine and the United States have gathered in Saudi Arabia to discuss a potential limited ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Greenland’s opposition party pushes closer US ties, independence from Denmark as Trump plays big in election
item.description: Greenland holds a parliamentary vote in an election that will likely reveal how the nation will seek independence from Denmark as well its response to President Trump's ambitions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump labels ex-Australian PM Malcolm Turnbull 'weak' amid spat
item.description: The president's attack on Turnbull came after comments Turnbull made to Bloomberg that Donald Trump's style is an "advantage" to China.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctors declare Pope Francis no longer in imminent danger due to pneumonia, but remains hospitalized
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors said Monday Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a month, but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader Sunday as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario will tariff electricity going to 3 US states on Monday, premier says
item.description: The leader said new tariffs are scheduled to be implemented Monday in an effort to push back on Trump’s international economic measures.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill more than 20 as Polish prime minister warns against appeasement
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched heavy aerial attacks on Ukraine for a second night Saturday after the United States stopped sharing satellite images with the Ukrainian government, officials said. At least 22 people have been killed. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine's energy supplies as US cuts its access to satellite images
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia attacked Ukrainian energy facilities with dozens of missiles and drones during the night, officials said Friday, hobbling the country’s ability to deliver heat and light to its citizens and to power weapons factories vital to its defenses.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization
item.description: Francis offered a first public sign of just how weak he is on Thursday by recording an audio message that was broadcast to the faithful in St. Peter’s Square.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Unexploded WWII bomb in Paris halts Eurostar travel to London, Brussels
item.description: Eurostar announced the cancellation of all its high-speed trains connecting Paris to London and Brussels on Friday after the discovery of a World War-II era bomb near the tracks serving Eurostar’s hub in the French capital, Gare du Nord.
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL PETREQUIN and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EU leaders commit to working together after Trump signals that Europe must defend itself
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders on Thursday committed to working together to bolster the continent's defenses and to free up hundreds of billions of euros for security after U.S. President Donald Trump’s repeated warnings that he would cut them adrift to face the threat of Russia alone.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9
